apps.cloud_policy: No broad DB speedup claim until compact_summary is rerun on RTX hardware and native phase counters prove materialization is not dominant.
apps.required_action: Use compact prepared-kernel outputs where the app needs counts/summaries, then add native phase counters proving Python is orchestration only.

apps.cloud_policy: Cloud only in the combined Goal889/905 graph gate; no graph RT-core claim until visibility, native BFS, and native triangle-count row digests pass on RTX hardware.
apps.required_action: Use visibility_edges any-hit plus explicit native BFS/triangle graph-ray candidate generation as the bounded graph-to-RT lowerings; keep CPU-side frontier bookkeeping and neighbor-set intersection outside the RT-core claim.

apps.cloud_policy: Never include in NVIDIA cloud batches.
apps.required_action: Keep as Apple Metal/MPS RT evidence; do not fold into NVIDIA OptiX claim tables.

apps.cloud_policy: No new pod is needed for readiness; use the Goal917 artifact in claim-review packaging, and rerun only as part of a consolidated regression batch.
apps.required_action: Keep the prepared OptiX gap-summary path as the RT-core claim path, and prevent row output or nearest-clinic output from being presented as the claim.

apps.cloud_policy: Cloud only after local profiler/baseline packaging is complete, and then only as a bounded RTX artifact batch; do not restart paid pods per app.
apps.required_action: Keep the prepared OptiX count-summary path bounded, then collect and review a real RTX phase artifact against the completed local baseline set before any claim.

apps.cloud_policy: Cloud only after the facility coverage-threshold profiler and baselines are packaged; no ranked KNN assignment RT-core claim.
apps.required_action: Use coverage_threshold_prepared for traversal-backed service-coverage decisions; KNN ranking and fallback assignment remain outside the RT-core claim until a native ranking design exists.

apps.cloud_policy: Cloud only in the focused native road-hazard gate after local packaging is complete; no road-hazard speedup claim until that passes.
apps.required_action: Run the explicit native segment/polygon mode through Goal888 strict RTX gating before changing default OptiX behavior or claims.

apps.cloud_policy: Cloud only in a focused Goal807 native-vs-host-indexed batch after local gate packaging is complete.
apps.required_action: Use Goal807 to prove explicit native hit-count correctness/performance against host-indexed and PostGIS where available before promotion.

apps.cloud_policy: Cloud only after the deferred Goal873 strict gate has a prepared batch slot; require row-digest parity, zero overflow, and independent review on RTX hardware.
apps.required_action: Run the explicit native bounded pair-row app mode through Goal873 strict RTX validation, then decide whether to promote the default public rows path or keep native mode as an explicit bounded gate.

apps.cloud_policy: Cloud only in the deferred Goal877 phase-profiler batch; no full polygon-area speedup claim.
apps.required_action: Keep OptiX native-assisted LSI/PIP candidate discovery split from CPU exact area refinement; collect a real RTX phase artifact before any claim.

apps.cloud_policy: Cloud only in the deferred Goal877 phase-profiler batch; no full Jaccard speedup claim.
apps.required_action: Keep OptiX native-assisted LSI/PIP candidate discovery split from CPU exact set-area/Jaccard refinement; collect a real RTX phase artifact before any claim.

apps.cloud_policy: Cloud only after the threshold-decision profiler and baselines are packaged; no exact Hausdorff distance RT-core claim.
apps.required_action: Use directed_threshold_prepared for traversal-backed Hausdorff decision workloads; exact-distance KNN rows remain outside the RT-core claim until a native ranking design exists.

apps.cloud_policy: Cloud only after the candidate-threshold profiler and baselines are packaged; no full ANN or ranking RT-core claim.
apps.required_action: Use candidate_threshold_prepared for traversal-backed candidate-coverage decisions; candidate-subset KNN ranking remains outside the RT-core claim until a native ranking design exists.

apps.cloud_policy: Include in the next batched cloud run only with the prepared scalar profiler.
apps.required_action: Keep prepared scalar threshold-count as the RT-core claim path and prevent default row mode from being presented as the claim.

apps.cloud_policy: Include in the next batched cloud run only with the prepared scalar profiler.
apps.required_action: Keep prepared scalar core-threshold summary as the RT-core claim path and split Python cluster expansion from native timing.

apps.cloud_policy: Include in the next batched cloud run with prepared packed input and scalar pose-count mode.
apps.required_action: Keep prepared ray/triangle any-hit scalar pose-count as the flagship RT-core path and expand only with phase-clean profilers.

apps.cloud_policy: Cloud only after the Barnes-Hut node-coverage profiler and baselines are packaged; no force-vector or opening-rule RT-core claim.
apps.required_action: Use node_coverage_prepared for traversal-backed node-coverage decisions; candidate rows, opening-rule evaluation, and force reduction remain outside the RT-core claim until native reduction designs exist.

apps.cloud_policy: Never include in NVIDIA OptiX cloud batches.
apps.required_action: Keep as HIPRT-specific validation; do not fold into NVIDIA OptiX app maturity.

boundary: This plan defines the v1.0 NVIDIA RT-core migration order. It is a planning artifact, not a release authorization and not a public speedup claim.
bucketing_note: Priority buckets are execution buckets, not pure status buckets. An app can already be rt_core_ready and still appear in must_finish_first when it is a flagship path with required optimization or claim-packaging work; robot_collision_screening is the current example.

acceptance: Every public app is assigned to a v1.0 bucket.
Each bucket has an explicit engineering purpose and claim boundary.
The plan is machine-readable and reviewable.
goal_series.scope: Use the canonical app maturity matrix to define promotion order, acceptance rules, and claim boundaries.
goal_series.title: Lock the v1.0 all-in-RT app migration plan

acceptance: Dry-run phase packet exists for both apps.
Prepared OptiX summary modes remain guarded by --require-rt-core.
Cloud inclusion criteria are explicit and bounded.
goal_series.scope: Package local evidence for service coverage and event hotspot prepared OptiX summary paths using existing profiler and CLI guards.
goal_series.title: Promote spatial prepared-summary apps to promotion-ready candidates

acceptance: Prepared DB compact-summary path has phase-clean counters.
Internal review package shows the dominant app path is no longer materialization-driven.
Claim remains bounded to prepared DB summary paths.
goal_series.scope: Push compact prepared DB outputs and native phase accounting until Python is orchestration only.
goal_series.title: Reduce DB app host/interface overhead on the OptiX path

acceptance: Explicit native mode passes strict correctness gate.
Compact summary/count outputs are separated from pair-row output.
No row-output claim is made without a native row emitter.
goal_series.scope: Run strict native-vs-host-indexed gating for hit-count and compact outputs before any row-output claim.
goal_series.title: Promote segment/polygon compact native OptiX paths

acceptance: Strict RTX artifact proves row-digest parity for visibility, native BFS, and native triangle-count sub-paths.
Host-indexed fallback remains the default until the native graph-ray path passes review.
Shortest-path, graph database, distributed graph analytics, and whole-app graph-system claims remain excluded.
goal_series.scope: Run the combined graph gate for visibility any-hit plus explicit native BFS/triangle graph-ray candidate generation on RTX hardware.
goal_series.title: Validate graph analytics native RT-core sub-paths

acceptance: Each app has a true traversal design or a permanent non-RT-core decision.
Public docs stop conflating CUDA-through-OptiX with RT-core use.
goal_series.consensus_requirement: 3-AI because it changes flagship app scope
goal_series.scope: Hausdorff, ANN candidate search, and Barnes-Hut require true RT traversal formulations or permanent non-RT-core classification.
goal_series.title: Redesign CUDA-through-OptiX apps into true traversal apps or demote them permanently

acceptance: Each app has a surface decision with rationale.
Any new OptiX surface has a local correctness gate.
goal_series.scope: Facility KNN, polygon overlap, and polygon Jaccard need either real OptiX surfaces or explicit retirement from NVIDIA RT-core targets.
goal_series.title: Expose or explicitly retire missing OptiX app surfaces

acceptance: All active-candidate apps have local readiness packets.
The cloud run is one batched session with preserved artifacts.
Interpretation stays bounded to exact measured sub-paths.
goal_series.scope: Use the single-session cloud procedure only after local work is ready.
goal_series.title: Run one consolidated RTX cloud validation batch after local closure
